source_paper: Neurotoxicity Following Exposure to Micro and Nanoplastics

critical_errors.detail: No TissueOrgan nodes exist, yet observations and biomarkers richly describe organ-level distribution (mouse brain, liver, kidney, gut; bivalve gills, hemolymph, digestive gland). Biomarkers carry a 'tissue_matrix' attribute (e.g., 'mouse brain tissue', 'mouse liver') but there is no canonical TissueOrgan node and no 'measured_in' or 'affects' edge.
critical_errors.evidence: Paper: '...particles in the mice's gut, liver, and kidneys...'; '...within the hemolymph, gills, and intestines...'. Ontology supports Biomarker->measured_in->TissueOrgan and Mechanism->affects->TissueOrgan.

critical_errors.detail: size_50_nm, size_40_nm, size_0_11_um, size_0_1_to_5_um, size_1_0_um, and shape_spherical have zero incident edges. They inflate node_count without participating in the graph.
critical_errors.evidence: No has_size_class / has_shape edge references these node ids.

critical_errors.issue: Size entities not found in provided paper content
critical_errors.detail: size_50_nm (50 nm) and size_0_11_um (0.11 um) do not appear in the supplied paper excerpt. 0.11 um may be a misextraction of the shrimp study's 0.1 um.
critical_errors.evidence: Provided sizes in text: 0.1-5, 1.0, 100-200, 1, 10, 20, 100, 1-5, 0.1 um and 40 nm. 50 nm and 0.11 um are absent (verify against full/truncated sections before keeping).

unsupported_entities.reason: No mention of 50 nm in provided paper content; orphan node. Verify against full text or remove.
0.11 um not present in provided content; likely a misread of the 0.1 um shrimp particle. Orphan node.
Duplicate of size_1_um (both min=max=1 um). Controlled-vocabulary duplication; orphan node.
Maps 'spherical' to label 'other' but is never linked to any observation (C. elegans 'spherical polystyrene' is not connected). Orphan node.

unsupported_entities.note: Biomarkers superoxide_dismutase_activity, catalase_activity, glutathione_s_transferase_activity, malondialdehyde_levels cite refs ([87],[89]-[93]) that fall in paper sections NOT included in the provided excerpt; their direction_of_change could not be confirmed from supplied text. Flag for source verification rather than asserting unsupported.

missing_entities.evidence: Abstract + Conclusions: 'exposure routes like inhalation, ingestion, and retrograde transport after nasal exposure'; particles 'taken in by aquatic beings and mammals'.
Section 5: caspase-3 is 'a marker for neuron cell apoptosis'; biomarker_caspase_3 exists but is linked to no Mechanism.

examples: European seabass [42]: reduced AChE release, oxidative stress, lipid peroxidation, anaerobic shift, irregular swimming
missing_entities.evidence: Section 5 reports this explicitly; not represented as observations/associations.

examples: disrupted neuron arrangement in cerebral cortex
missing_entities.evidence: Section 5 describes these morphological findings; not captured.
Zero Sample nodes; Observations are not 'observed_in' any Sample.

unsupported_relationships.detail: All 105 edges use valid relation types with ontology-compliant source/target types (reports, supports, evaluates, assesses, identified_by, produces, has_polymer, has_size_class, has_shape, links, supported_by, evidenced_by, evaluated_in).

Both directions are stored for the same relation (e.g., Study--supports-->Association AND Association--supported_by-->Study; Observation--identified_by-->Method AND Method--produces-->Observation), inflating edge_count. Not invalid, but redundant.

missing_relationships.detail: oxidative_stress -> neurological_disorders is only expressed via an Association; the direct ontology edge is unused.

C. elegans (0.1-5 um, spherical polystyrene, 1 mg/L) and the rat study (40 nm PS) have Associations but no Observation, so size_0_1_to_5_um, size_40_nm, shape_spherical, polystyrene remain unlinked for those studies.
biomarker_dopamine_levels and biomarker_caspase_3 connect only to the Study (evaluated_in); neither is tied to a Mechanism or Association despite clear interpretive context.

ontology_violations.detail: Several Observation nodes set unit to 'presence reported', 'reported direction', or 'reported null finding' rather than a measurement unit.
size_0_1_um (0.1 um = 100 nm) and size_0_11_um are tagged bin_type 'micro' though they sit at/below the nano boundary; size_50_nm and size_40_nm are 'nano' but omit min/max_micrometer.

Field holds narrative findings (e.g., 'confirmed absorption and presence of particles in freeze-dried tissues') rather than a description of the method's detection capability. Within schema type but semantically off.

observation_association_errors.detail: Measured findings are Observation nodes; interpretations/suggestions are Association nodes. Good overall.

observation_association_errors.issue: Triple representation of the null genotoxicity finding
observation_association_errors.detail: The single 'no genotoxic effects in Dreissena polymorpha' result appears as an Observation (obs_zebra_mussel_no_genotoxic_effects), an Association (assoc_zebra_mussel_genotoxicity_null), AND a Mechanism (mechanism_genotoxicity). Verify this is intentional and not double counting.
Observations encode 'increased'/'upregulated' inside value with metric_type presence_absence; acceptable but borders on interpretation.

quantitative_errors.detail: Paper states '...plateau at about 0.8' without restating mg/g; extraction assigns mg/g. Verify.

quantitative_errors.issue: Exposure doses/concentrations not captured as structured quantities
quantitative_errors.detail: Held only in notes: mouse 0.01-0.5 mg/day (~0.5-25 mg/kg/day); rat 1-10 mg/kg/day, 40 nm, 5 weeks; C. elegans 1 mg/L; zebra mussel 1 and 4x10^6 MPs/L; earthworm 0.1-1.5 g/kg soil; shrimp 0.001-10 mg/L; Corbicula 0.2/0.7 mg/L.
Extracted tissue concentrations (0.2, 1.0, 1.4 mg/g for 5 um in liver/kidney/gut) match the paper exactly.

study_structure_errors.issue: Primary studies collapsed into one review Study
study_structure_errors.detail: All observations/associations attach to the single review node via reports/supports. Primary references ([41],[42],[85],[86], etc.) are preserved only as biomarker citations_ref, so most observations lack per-study provenance.
Observations are not linked to Sample nodes (Observation->observed_in->Sample chain absent).
supports/supported_by and identified_by/produces are both stored, so 105 edges overstate the number of unique relations.

recommendations: Add TissueOrgan nodes (brain, cerebral cortex, liver, kidney, gut, gills, hemolymph, digestive gland) and connect via Biomarker->measured_in and Mechanism->affects.
Verify 50 nm and 0.11 um against the full/truncated text; remove if unsupported and merge size_1_0_um into size_1_um.
Connect or remove orphan nodes: link size_40_nm to the rat study and size_0_1_to_5_um / shape_spherical to the C. elegans finding (add the missing Observation), or drop them.
Add ExposurePathway nodes (oral, inhalation, nasal/retrograde) with limited-evidence hedging, sourced from abstract/conclusions.
Represent the European seabass [42] findings and the neurodegeneration / nuclear pyknosis observations from Section 5.
Fix Observation.unit misuse (use a real unit or move descriptors to notes) and add structured exposure-dose observations.
Preserve provenance: model key primary studies as separate Study nodes or add a citation attribute on observations.
Link caspase_3 to an apoptosis Mechanism (label 'other') and tie dopamine_levels to a neurotransmitter-related Mechanism/Association.
Optionally deduplicate inverse-pair edges to keep edge_count meaningful.
